review_text:
Might be one of our favourite campgrounds in Australia so far. Stayed at site 6, right by the beach. Very quiet, heaps of space between sites and good tree cover too. Shady.
Campfires allowed (follow fire danger rules and park rules) either in communal pits or in private above ground pits.
Beach is glorious! Shallow, but the water is so clear.
Great view of sunrise over the headland too. And dark sky, so good for star watching.
Wonderful small campground in the Lincoln National Park about a half hours drive from Port Lincoln on bitumen all the way.

Clean area,  drop toilet area clean.  Very spacious area. Unfortunately many sites not very suitable to tents or swags. There was a lot of gravel/stones put in majority of sites. It appeared randomly put out.  It really made it a challenge to find a site to put a tent on.  I think this needs to be addressed,  otherwise state on the website that it's a gravelled area. The natural beauty of the area is definitely worth the drive out there.
Beautiful clear warm ocean water, march flies can be painful wear light coloured clothing dark colours will attract them.

Awesome spot at Lincoln National Park.
Easy drive in on sealed roads with ample sites available for a variety of trailers, motorhome and even large caravans.
We used this as our base for visiting the bulk of the sights and places of interest at the park.
I didn't camp here, simply visited for the day and if you are looking to camp here you can't go wrong. One of many great places in the NP
We stayed at site 16 and had a great water view. Site 13 has the best view. Water was warm enough to swim in late April. Walks and views in park are spectacular.

Great campground, only a couple of others here at the moment but not in line of sight. Plenty of foliage around so it feels very private.  Campsites 1-7 are right by the beach but mostly shaded. Up the hill a little the rest get full sun (great for those solar panels) and a view across to Cape Donington. The best site would be #13, while #14 looks good on the map a tree obscures the view. We are in #16 and have a terrific view. The cleanest long drop toilet I've ever seen is just nearby.
Nice beautiful and natural peaceful place .unluckey  me camp site was full when i went there😉

My husband and I stayed here for 5 nights in our caravan.  It is a very peaceful spot, being in Port Lincoln National Park.  You need to be self-sufficient as there is no water or power here.  There is 1 drop toilet, though.  The beautiful beach is a short walk & you can see it from your campsite if you are lucky enough to be on sites 3, 4, 5, or 6.   We had a great stay.
No rubbish bins people very noisy,not happy.
